summary.title: Scenario 3 - Microservices Healthcare Application
summary.description: A healthcare microservices patient management system with Patient Portal, API Gateway, Identity Service, Medical Records Service, Appointment Service, and Notification Service.

data.name: Public Internet
data.description: Untrusted external zone. All traffic from here is treated as hostile until authenticated and authorised.

data.name: DMZ - Perimeter Zone
data.description: Perimeter zone containing only the Patient Portal and API Gateway. These are the only two components exposed to the public internet. Acts as the security boundary between untrusted internet traffic and all internal services.

data.name: Internal Services Zone
data.description: Trusted internal network hosting all backend microservices. Not directly reachable from the public internet. All inbound traffic must pass through the API Gateway.

data.name: Third-Party Providers Zone
data.description: External untrusted zone hosting third-party SMS and email delivery providers used by the Notification Service. All outbound communications crossing this boundary must be encrypted.

data.name: Patient
data.description: End user accessing the healthcare portal from a browser over the public internet to view medical records and book appointments

data.name: Patient Portal
data.description: Web frontend exposed to the public internet. Patients log in, view medical records, and book appointments. All backend calls go through the API Gateway.

threats.title: Patient Portal Phishing and Credential Harvesting
threats.description: An attacker creates a convincing replica of the patient portal login page and tricks patients into submitting credentials via phishing emails. Captured credentials are used to impersonate patients and access their medical records.
threats.mitigation: Enforce multi-factor authentication for all patient accounts. Implement HSTS and certificate pinning. Monitor for login anomalies such as unusual geolocations. Align with OWASP Top 10 A07 Identification and Authentication Failures.

threats.title: Cross-Site Scripting on Patient Portal
threats.description: An attacker injects malicious JavaScript through unsanitised input fields such as appointment notes. When other patients or staff view the page the script executes, stealing session tokens or redirecting users to malicious sites.
threats.mitigation: Implement a strict Content Security Policy header. Sanitise and HTML-encode all user-supplied output server-side. Set HttpOnly and Secure flags on all session cookies. Perform regular penetration testing of frontend input fields.

data.name: API Gateway
data.description: Single entry point for all inbound requests to internal microservices. Routes authenticated requests to appropriate backend services and enforces access control policies.

threats.title: API Gateway DDoS Attack
threats.description: An attacker floods the API Gateway with high-volume requests, exhausting its processing capacity. Since all internal services are routed exclusively through the gateway, a successful attack makes the entire patient management system unavailable.
threats.mitigation: Deploy rate limiting and throttling per client IP and per authenticated user. Integrate a cloud-based DDoS mitigation service. Implement auto-scaling and circuit breakers. Align with NIST 800-53 SC-5 Denial of Service Protection.

threats.title: Internal Endpoints Exposed via Gateway Misconfiguration
threats.description: A misconfigured routing rule or overly permissive policy inadvertently exposes internal medical record endpoints to the public internet without authentication. An attacker enumerating API paths could retrieve protected health information with no valid credentials.
threats.mitigation: Apply a whitelist-only approach to API routing, denying all unlisted paths by default. Enforce JWT validation at the gateway before routing any request. Conduct regular configuration audits and automated security scanning. Align with OWASP API Security Top 10 API3.

data.name: Identity Service
data.description: Handles authentication and authorisation using OAuth 2.0. Issues signed JSON Web Tokens to authenticated users. All internal services verify tokens through this service.

threats.title: JWT Token Forgery via Weak Signing Key
threats.description: If the Identity Service uses a weak or default secret key to sign JWTs, an attacker can forge tokens granting any patient identity or administrative role. All internal services accepting these tokens without proper verification will process malicious requests as authenticated.
threats.mitigation: Use RS256 asymmetric signing with a minimum 2048-bit RSA key pair. Store private keys in an HSM or secrets manager. Rotate signing keys periodically. Implement short JWT expiry times. All services must validate token signature, issuer, audience, and expiry on every request.

threats.title: OAuth Scope Escalation via Identity Service
threats.description: An attacker manipulates the OAuth 2.0 request to claim broader scopes than entitled, for example requesting write access to medical records when only read access should be granted. An overly permissive Identity Service issues the inflated JWT, granting excess permissions across all internal services.
threats.mitigation: Enforce strict server-side scope validation, ignoring client-requested scopes not pre-approved. Implement least privilege for all OAuth scopes. Log all token issuance events with requested and granted scopes for audit purposes.

data.name: Medical Records Service
data.description: Stores and retrieves patient medical records including diagnoses, prescriptions, and test results. Verifies JWTs with the Identity Service before processing any request.

threats.title: Broken Object Level Authorisation on Patient Records
threats.description: The Medical Records Service does not enforce object-level authorisation. An authenticated patient can access another patient records by modifying the patient ID in the request. This allows mass exposure of protected health information for any patient in the system.
threats.mitigation: Implement mandatory object-level authorisation for every record operation, verifying the requesting user identity in the JWT matches the patient ID of the requested record. Apply OWASP API Security Top 10 API1 Broken Object Level Authorisation controls.

threats.title: Medical Record Tampering via Compromised Service Account
threats.description: An attacker compromising an internal service account can send authenticated requests to modify or delete patient records, alter diagnoses, or remove prescriptions. Without integrity controls, tampered data is indistinguishable from legitimate records, posing a direct patient safety risk.
threats.mitigation: Enforce role-based access control separating read-only patient access from write access reserved for clinical staff. Implement cryptographic signing of medical record entries. Enable immutable audit logging for all create, update, and delete operations. Follow NIST 800-53 SI-7.

threats.title: Absence of Medical Record Access Audit Logs
threats.description: Without immutable audit logging a malicious insider who accesses or modifies patient records can deny having done so. This also makes it impossible to satisfy HIPAA audit control requirements or investigate data breaches.
threats.mitigation: Implement append-only audit logging for all access and modification events, capturing user identity, timestamp, action, record ID, and source IP. Store logs in a separate tamper-evident store. Retain logs as required by healthcare regulations. Align with ISO 27001 A.12.4.

data.name: Appointment Service
data.description: Manages patient appointment scheduling and cancellations. Reads patient data from the Medical Records Service and triggers reminders via the Notification Service.

threats.title: Appointment Changes without Non-Repudiation
threats.description: The Appointment Service does not maintain a verifiable record of who booked, modified, or cancelled an appointment. A rogue staff member could cancel critical appointments and deny responsibility with no audit evidence, posing both a patient safety risk and a compliance failure.
threats.mitigation: Log all appointment events with authenticated user identity, timestamp, and change details in an immutable audit trail. Implement digital signatures on appointment records for cryptographic non-repudiation. Align with HIPAA audit control requirements under 45 CFR 164.312.

data.name: Notification Service
data.description: Sends appointment reminders to patients via SMS and email using third-party provider APIs. Receives notification requests from the Appointment Service.

threats.title: Patient Health Information Leakage in Notification Payloads
threats.description: The Notification Service includes excessive patient health information such as diagnosis details in SMS or email payloads sent to third-party providers. Once this data crosses the trust boundary the organisation loses control, risking PHI exposure in violation of HIPAA if the provider is breached.
threats.mitigation: Enforce data minimisation in all outbound notifications, including only appointment date and time. Never include diagnosis or medication details. Encrypt all payloads in transit using TLS 1.2 or higher. Review third-party agreements for HIPAA compliance. Apply NIST 800-53 SC-8.

threats.title: Notification Service Abuse for Message Flooding
threats.description: An attacker exploiting a vulnerability in the Appointment Service can trigger the Notification Service to send large volumes of SMS or email messages, exhausting third-party provider API quotas. This disables legitimate appointment reminders for all patients and may incur significant financial cost.
threats.mitigation: Implement strict per-user and per-appointment rate limits on notification dispatch. Validate notification recipients match the patient identity in the JWT. Monitor third-party API usage and alert on anomalous volume spikes. Use message queues with backpressure controls.

data.name: SMS Provider
data.description: Third-party external SMS delivery service used by the Notification Service to send appointment reminder text messages to patients

data.name: Email Provider
data.description: Third-party external email delivery service used by the Notification Service to send appointment confirmation and reminder emails to patients

data.name: Portal Access (HTTPS)
data.description: Patient accesses the portal login page and submits credentials over HTTPS from the public internet

data.name: Portal Response (HTTPS)
data.description: Patient Portal returns requested information or confirmation to the patient browser over HTTPS

data.name: Forward API Request
data.description: Patient Portal forwards authenticated patient requests to the API Gateway for routing to internal backend services

data.name: Token Validation Request
data.description: API Gateway forwards JWT to the Identity Service for signature and claims verification before routing any request to internal services

data.name: JWT Validation Result
data.description: Identity Service returns validated claims and authorisation result to the API Gateway

data.name: Medical Record Request
data.description: API Gateway routes authenticated patient medical record read request to the Medical Records Service

data.name: Appointment Request
data.description: API Gateway routes authenticated appointment booking or viewing request to the Appointment Service

data.name: Verify JWT Token
data.description: Medical Records Service independently verifies the JWT with the Identity Service before returning any patient data

data.name: Read Patient Data
data.description: Appointment Service retrieves relevant patient data from the Medical Records Service to support appointment scheduling

data.name: Trigger Reminder
data.description: Appointment Service sends appointment details to the Notification Service to dispatch reminder messages to the patient

data.name: SMS Reminder (HTTPS)
data.description: Notification Service sends appointment reminder SMS request to the third-party SMS provider via HTTPS API

data.name: Email Reminder (SMTP)
data.description: Notification Service sends appointment reminder email to the third-party email provider via SMTP or HTTPS API
